news.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.4 with Native 'Computer Use' Capabilities
gist: Introduces native ability for the AI to operate software, keyboards, and mice directly.
Features a massive 1-million-token context window and 33% reduction in factual errors.
news.full_summary: OpenAI officially released GPT-5.4 and GPT-5.4 Pro on March 5, 2026. This next-generation model moves beyond text to 'agentic' actions, allowing it to interact with web interfaces and desktop software as a human would. It includes a specialized 'Thinking' variant for complex reasoning and is significantly more token-efficient than its predecessors.
news.tip: Use the new 'Thinking' variant for tasks requiring multi-step planning rather than just information retrieval.

news.title: Anthropic Sues Pentagon Over 'National Security Risk' Blacklist
gist: Anthropic was blacklisted after refusing to remove safety guardrails for military use.
The lawsuit alleges First and Fifth Amendment violations by the U.S. government.
news.full_summary: On March 9, 2026, Anthropic filed a landmark lawsuit against the U.S. Department of Defense. The conflict erupted when Anthropic refused to allow its Claude models to be used for autonomous lethal weapons or mass surveillance. The Pentagon subsequently labeled the company a 'supply chain risk,' a designation typically reserved for foreign adversaries.
news.tip: Monitor this case closely as it will define the legal boundaries between corporate AI safety policies and national security mandates.

news.title: Nvidia Releases Nemotron 3 Super as 120B Open-Weight Powerhouse
gist: A hybrid Mamba-2/Transformer model optimized for the new Blackwell architecture.
Nvidia released the weights, training recipes, and a 10-trillion-token dataset.
news.full_summary: Nvidia disrupted the 'closed-model' dominance on March 11, 2026, by releasing Nemotron 3 Super. This 120B parameter model is designed for high-throughput agentic reasoning. By providing the full pretraining dataset and post-training recipes, Nvidia is positioning itself as the primary infrastructure and model provider for the open AI community.
news.tip: Deploy this model via NVIDIA NIM or Ollama for high-performance, private agentic workflows.

news.title: Google Unveils 'Antigravity' Agent-First Development Platform
gist: A new IDE and platform designed specifically for orchestrating autonomous AI agents.
Features 'Mission Control' for managing multiple agents working in parallel on code and web tasks.
news.full_summary: Google launched the public preview of Antigravity, a platform that shifts the focus from simple code completion to autonomous execution. Developers can now deploy agents that plan, execute, and verify software tasks across integrated editors, terminals, and browsers, providing verifiable 'Artifacts' of their work.
news.tip: Switch from manual prompting to 'Mission Control' in Antigravity to automate end-to-end feature development.

news.title: Meta and AMD Sign Massive $60 Billion AI Infrastructure Deal
gist: Meta will deploy 6 gigawatts of custom AMD Instinct GPUs to diversify away from Nvidia.
The deal includes an equity component, giving Meta a potential 10% stake in AMD.
news.full_summary: In a strategic move to secure its hardware future, Meta announced a $60 billion partnership with AMD. The deal centers on custom MI450 GPUs and 6th Gen EPYC CPUs. This massive buildout aims to support Mark Zuckerberg's goal of 'personal superintelligence' while reducing Meta's dependency on a single hardware vendor.
news.tip: Watch for improved performance in Meta's open-source Llama models as they are optimized for this specific hardware stack.

news.title: Grammarly Faces Class-Action Lawsuit Over AI 'Identity Cloning'
gist: Plaintiffs allege Grammarly 'cloned' expert styles for its AI feature without consent.
The 'Expert Review' feature was suspended immediately following the legal filing.
news.full_summary: Grammarly was hit with a major lawsuit on March 11, 2026, led by journalist Julia Angwin. The complaint alleges that the company's 'Expert Review' tool used the names and reputations of real-world experts, including Stephen King and Kara Swisher, to market AI-generated feedback as if it were endorsed by those individuals.
news.tip: Ensure your AI implementations avoid using real-world personas or names without explicit licensing to prevent 'Right of Publicity' claims.

tools.name: Workbeaver
tools.description: A 'digital twin' desktop agent that learns to automate any workflow by watching you perform it once.

tools.name: Claude Code
tools.description: Anthropic's CLI-native agent that lets you build, test, and deploy entire applications via terminal-based 'vibe coding'.

tools.name: Luma Creative Agents
tools.description: End-to-end AI agents for high-fidelity video, 3D modeling, and creative asset generation in a single interface.

tools.name: CodeAnt AI
tools.description: An AI 'bottleneck breaker' that automates code reviews and security scanning to manage the surge of AI-generated PRs.

tools.name: NotebookLM Audio Overview 2.0
tools.description: Google's updated research tool that creates interactive, multi-person podcast summaries of massive document sets.

tools.name: Cursor BugBot
tools.description: A proactive AI agent within the Cursor editor that finds and fixes logic errors in real-time as you type.

meme.description: The viral 'Shape Store' video used a surreal 90s camcorder aesthetic to prove AI video can move beyond 'slop' into genuine artistic merit, sparking a massive debate on X about the future of creativity.
